title: Arbitrary Filesystem Enumeration via API

cvss_v3_1.notes: C:L because only directory/file names are disclosed, not file contents. Scored independently; chained with RADARR-001 the impact is higher.

cwe: CWE-548: Exposure of Information Through Directory Listing
description:
The /api/v3/filesystem endpoint accepts an arbitrary path parameter and returns the directory contents (subdirectories and files) for any location on the filesystem. There is no restriction to Radarr's data or media directories. An authenticated attacker can enumerate the entire filesystem structure including sensitive paths like /etc, /home, /root, /var, etc.

When combined with RADARR-001 (CORS), this is exploitable from any website that knows the API key, using the ?apikey= query parameter (no preflight required).

root_cause.detail: No path restriction or allowlist. Any filesystem path accepted.

remediation: Restrict the filesystem endpoint to paths within configured root folders and the Radarr data directory
Alternatively, implement an allowlist of browseable parent directories
